growth_records.medium: Modified artificial seawater (ASW) medium, P-free base + P source as indicated
growth_records.cite: Westermann et al. Bacterial catabolism of membrane phospholipids links marine biogeochemical cycles. Science Advances (2023). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.adf5122. [PMC10132767]
growth_records.snippet: grew on 173.2 uM exogenous PC or PE ... (mu = 0.255, 0.121, and 0.171 hour-1 for Pi, PC, and PE grown cultures, respectively) [Results, Fig. 1A]
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate mu directly reported in text for cultures grown on Pi (K2HPO4) (173.2 uM) as sole P source, following 48 h P-starvation, in modified ASW medium with 10 mM sodium succinate as carbon source, 30C, shaking. Doubling time calculated as ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.255 = 2.718 h.

growth_records.medium: Marine Basal Medium + 3 mM ammonium + phytoplankton metabolite (12 mM carbon)

up.units: acts as growth substrate (significant growth 24 h); numeric value in Dataset S1

growth_records.cite: Clerc et al. Chemotaxis, growth, and inter-species interactions shape early bacterial community assembly. ISME J (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf101. [PMC12202767]
growth_records.snippet: DMSP played a role as both a signal ... and a substrate for three strains-Thalassospira sp. HF15, Phaeobacter sp. HF9A, and Pseudoalteromonas sp. HF66 (Fig. 1A, t-tests, P <= 0.05, Dataset S1-S2)
growth_records.notes: Same assay as HF15 record. No numeric growth rate in text (Dataset S1/Fig 1 only). Text confirms DMSP is a growth substrate for Phaeobacter sp. HF9A. Medium base not itemized -> stated=false. No arithmetic performed.
